concept: Stubborn / Refusing to Change / Fixed in Position
description: Having or showing dogged determination not to change one's attitude or position. 'Turo' names the quality of stubbornness—persistence that can be strength or obstacle.
sound_symbolism: The word holds fast. 'Tu' plants with solid force, position fixed. Then 'ro' rolls against pressure, refusing to move.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'turo miona' (stubborn person). Related to 'kesu' (determined) but with connotation of inflexibility rather than purposeful resolve.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Stubbornness can be attachment—clinging to position rather than responding to what is.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Stubborn communication blocks resolution—flexibility serves understanding better than rigid positions.
